description:
This package provides a gui program as well as a rviz plugin to publish static transforms.
  Both support the transformation between various Euler angle representations.
  The rviz plugin also allows to configure the transform with an interactive marker.
readme: ROS package tools facilitating definition (static) ROS transforms. extensions rviz: EulerProperty class allowing definition arbitrary sets Euler angles conversion RotationProperty class combining EulerProperty QuaternionProperty provide flexible means entering orientation information. interactive transform publisher rviz::Display plugin allowing define transforms rviz interactive marker. interactively perform frame transformations. cmdline utilities: static_transform_publisher adapted version tf2_ros’s source code, providing fine-grained command-line options define orientations arbitrary set Euler angles. static_transform_publisher_gui interactive version static_transform_publisher allowing modify transform interactively.

This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

description:
A ROS wrapper of the AprilTag 3 visual fiducial detection
    algorithm. Provides full access to the core AprilTag 3 algorithm's
    customizations and makes the tag detection image and detected tags' poses
    available over ROS topics (including tf). The core AprilTag 3 algorithm is
    extended to allow the detection of tag bundles and a bundle calibration
    script is provided (bundle detection is more accurate than single tag
    detection). Continuous (camera image stream) and single image detector nodes
    are available.

readme: Beluga VDB Overview BelugaVDB library extension beluga integrates OpenVDB, enabling advanced localization capabilities. extension OpenVDB efficiently process maps pointcloud data. Mapping BelugaVDB requires level-set maps vdb format order work properly. VDB maps generated suitables party packages VDB Mapping, resulting map converted level-set map. simple post-processing code adapting map generated VDB Mapping vdb level-set map shown #include <openvdb/openvdb.h> #include <openvdb/tools/TopologyToLevelSet.h> int main() openvdb::initialize(); Create VDB file object. openvdb::io::File file("vdb_mapping_map.vdb"); Open file. reads file header, grids. file.open(); Print names grid (openvdb::io::File::NameIterator nameIter file.beginName(); nameIter file.endName(); ++nameIter) std::cout "Grid nameIter.gridName() std::endl; Retrieve shared pointer openvdb::GridBase::Ptr baseGrid; baseGrid file.readGrid("[0]"); Close file file.close(); Cast generic grid pointer FloatGrid pointer. openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid openvdb::gridPtrCast<openvdb::FloatGrid>(baseGrid); Transform level set openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid_levelset nullptr; grid_levelset openvdb::tools::topologyToLevelSet(*grid, Save grid openvdb::io::File("levle_set_map.vdb").write({grid_levelset}); vdb level-set maps generated pcd maps (created SLAM libraries FAST-LIO). simple code generate vdb level-set map pcd file shown ```cpp #include <openvdb/openvdb.h> #include <openvdb/tree/Tree.h> #include <openvdb/tools/TopologyToLevelSet.h> #include <openvdb/math/Transform.h> #include <pcl/io/pcd_io.h> #include <pcl/point_types.h> #include int main() openvdb::initialize(); pcl::PointCloud<pcl::PointXYZ>::Ptr cloud pcl::PointCloud<pcl::PointXYZ>); Open PCD file (pcl::io::loadPCDFile<pcl::PointXYZ> ("map.pcd", *cloud) load file PCL_ERROR read file map.pcd return std::cout "Loaded cloud succesfuly"<< std::endl; Create VDB grid resolution openvdb::FloatGrid::Ptr grid openvdb::FloatGrid::create(); grid->setTransform(openvdb::math::Transform::createLinearTransform(0.5)); grid->setGridClass(openvdb::GRID_LEVEL_SET); accessor coordinate-based access voxels. openvdb::FloatGrid::Accessor accessor grid->getAccessor(); Fill grid (const auto& point: *cloud) World coordinates openvdb::Vec3f world_vect(point.x, point.y, point.z); Transform index world openvdb::math::Transform transform; openvdb::math::Coord ijk transform.worldToIndexCellCentered(world_vect); File truncated lines full file

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing. The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

Python implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

Provides a toolchain running through the robot calibration process. This
     involves capturing calibration data, estimating parameters, and
     then updating the URDF.

Runs an optimization to estimate the a robot's kinematic parameters. This package is a
    generic rewrite of pr2_calibration_estimation.

This package contains a collection of launch files that can be helpful in configuring
    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

This package defines messages for storing calibration samples
     to be used in full robot calibration procedures. This package
     is still unstable. Expect the messages to change.

    which is based on Michael Ferguson's calibration code

Provide a node that extracts checkerboard corners from ROS images.
    This package is still experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Tools for calculating the intersection of interval messages coming
    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Provides a node that reports how long a subset of joints has been
     settled. That is, it calculates how long a set of joints has remained
     within a specified threshold. This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Extracts checkerboard corners from a dense laser snapshot.
     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

Listens on a ImageFeatures topic, and waits for the data to settle.
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Defines helper functions and routines that greatly help when trying to create a settler

description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

Based reply reply_directly. Set default results default_result bothering reply* time, actions expected success real robot, text-to-speech. override temporarily, syntax: scriptable_thing.custom_reply(): scriptable_thing.reply(SomeActionResult('non-default')) Examples scripts directory scripts: example_test.py dummy_behavior.py work dummy behavior wait commands robot charge plug test sends commands checks behavior handles command correctly. Run dummy_behavior rosrun scenario_test_tools dummy_behavior.py start test rosrun scenario_test_tools example_test.py --start_scenario=test_all move_base_success.py default_result feature move_base implementation succeeds time example_move_base elaborate version handling goal differently show features framework. goal received succeeds, aborted goals expected handled

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package calculates distances between both robot links and obstacles to be used for obstacle avoidance within cob_twist_controller framework.
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

build run tests debug build reasonable coverage numbers): CATKIN_MAKE: catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report CATKIN_TOOLS: catkin config --cmake-args -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin build catkin build PACKAGE_NAME --no-deps --catkin-make-args PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report output print coverage report located Python rostest Support interface Python-based unit tests require modification coverage information, Python-based nodes run rostest launch files bit additional instrumentation turned <launch> Add argument launch file turn coverage <arg name="coverage" default="false"/> fancy line forces nodes generate coverage <arg name="pythontest_launch_prefix" value="$(eval 'python-coverage run arg('coverage') node generate coverage information <node pkg="example_pkg" name="publisher_node" type="publisher_node.py" node generate coverage <node pkg="example_pkg" name="subscriber_node" type="subscriber_node.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" test generate coverage information include launch-prefix <test time-limit="10" test-name="sample_rostest" pkg="example_pkg" type="sample_rostest.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" </launch> CMakeLists, pass argument: add_rostest(example_rostest.test ARGS coverage:=ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING)

    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.

description: Calibration and removing of magnetometer bias.
readme: magnetometer_pipeline Calibration removing magnetometer bias. Node magnetometer_bias_remover nodelet magnetometer_pipeline/magnetometer_bias_remover magnetometer work correctly, required measure bias. node listens imu/mag_bias topic measurement, message arrives, node publish node publishing bias, alternatively provide parameters. Depending application, required re-estimate bias time time runtime. Subscribed topics imu/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): 3-axis magnetometer measurements (bias removed). imu/mag_bias (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): Bias magnetometer. subtracted incoming magnetometer measurements. Messages topic repeatedly bias change. magnetic_field_covariance field “misused” carry bias scaling matrix. Published topics explanation) imu/mag_unbiased (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): magnetic field measurement bias removed. remap topics namespace imu, sufficient remap imu:=new_imu. Parameters ~initial_mag_bias_x (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_mag_bias_y (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_mag_bias_z (double, default, optional): Magnetometer bias axis. ~initial_scaling_matrix (double[9], optional): Magnetometer scaling matrix (row-major). ~initial_mag_bias_* params, node receive bias messages. Node magnetometer_bias_observer Magnetometer bias estimation node. Subscribed topics imu/mag (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): raw magnetometer measurements. Published topics imu/mag_bias (sensor_msgs/MagneticField): estimated bias. speak/warn (std_msgs/String): Optional topic node reports user instructions. services calibrate_magnetometer (std_srvs/Trigger): Call service start bias estimation. Rotate robot axes calibration. Parameters ~measuring_time (double, default long bias estimation phase ~2d_mode (bool, default true): true, calibration expects motion axes ~2d_mode_ignore_axis default autodetect): magnetometer local axis calibration, set ~load_from_params (bool, default false): true, initial bias estimate loaded ROS params. magnetometer_bias_x (double, default initial bias estimate axis ~load_from_params true). magnetometer_bias_y (double, default initial bias estimate axis ~load_from_params true). magnetometer_bias_z (double, default initial bias estimate axis ~load_from_params true). ~load_from_file (bool, default true): true, initial bias estimate loaded ~/.ros/magnetometer_calib.yaml. ~calibration_file_path (str, default ~/.ros/magnetometer_calib.yaml): Path calibration file. ~save_to_file (bool, default true): true, estimated bias saved calibration file. MagnetometerBiasRemover class Helper class remove bias 3-axis magnetometer. BiasRemoverFilter message filter Message filter providing magnetometer measurements removed bias.

description: The CRANE-X7 bringup package

readme: crane_x7_control CRANE-X7のためのパッケージ、 crane_x7_ros のcontrolパッケージです。 crane_x7_controlはU2D2(USB-RS485変換ケーブル)を使用してCRANE-X7のモータ制御を行います。 U2D2はLinuxに接続すると/dev/ttyUSB0として認識されるので、次のコマンドでアクセス権限を変更します。 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 ケーブルの接続ポート名はデフォルトで/dev/ttyUSB0です。 /dev/ttyUSB1)を使う場合は次のコマンドを実行します。 roslaunch crane_x7_control crane_x7_control.launch port:=/dev/ttyUSB1 crane_x7_controlはデフォルト200Hz周期で制御しています。 制御周期を変更する場合はcrane_x7_control/src/hardware.cppを編集してください。 #define CONTROL_HZ rostopic /crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current subscribed [/crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current] average rate: min: max: std dev: window: average rate: min: max: std dev: window: 実際の制御周期が200Hzに達せず、100Hz程度に低くなってしまう場合、USB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更してください。 cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting crane_x7_controlは/crane_x7をルートとするネームスペースにパラメータやトピックを定義します。 各jointについて次のトピックを配信します。 current：電流値[mA] dxl_position：現在角度[360/4096度] temp：温度[度] dynamic_reconfigure crane_x7_controlはdynamic_reconfigureに対応しています。次のコマンドでrqt_reconfigureを起動してアクセスすると各Jointのサーボパラメータを変更することができます。 rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure 各パラメータの詳細についてはROBOTIS公式のXM430およびXM540のサーボマニュアルを参照して下さい。 XM430-W350 XM540-W270 CRANE-X7は位置制御モードと電流制御モードに対応しています。基本設定は位置制御モードになっていますが、複数箇所の設定変更を行うことで電流制御モードへ切り替えることが可能です。 サーボモータのOperating Mode（Address：11）を3：位置制御モードから0：電流制御モードへ設定変更する Dynamixel Wizard crane_x7_controlの設定変更 crane_x7_control/config/crane_x7_control.yamlのハンド部分について次のように変更する ```diff gripper_controller: type: description:

    registering variables
readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

description:
Dynamixel-Workbench is dynamixel solution for ROS.
    This metapackage allows you to easily change the ID, baudrate and operating mode of the Dynamixel.
    Furthermore, it supports various controllers based on operating mode and Dynamixel SDK.
    These controllers are commanded by operators.
This package contains examples of applying the 'dynamixel_workbench_toolbox' library developed on the basis of dynamixel_sdk to various operating modes of Dynamixel.
This package contains nodes that control the Dynamixel by communicating with the server registered in the 'dynamixel_workbench_controllers' package

This package is composed of 'dynamixel_item', 'dynamixel_tool', 'dynamixel_driver' and 'dynamixel_workbench' class.
    The 'dynamixel_item' is saved as control table item and information of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_tool' class loads its by model number of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_driver' class includes wraped function used in DYNAMIXEL SDK.
    The 'dynamixel_workbench' class make simple to use Dynamixels
readme: run dynamixel_workbench linux ROS Downloads DynamixelSDK git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/DynamixelSDK Build DynamixelSDK http://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_sdk/library_setup/cpp_linux/#build-the-library Downloads Dynamixel-Workbench git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel-workbench Build Dynamixel-Workbench Run examples ${YOUR_DOWNLOAD_PATH}/dynamixel_workbench/dynamixel_workbench_toolbox/examples mkdir build build cmake sudo chmod a+rw ${USB_PORT} ./monitor

description: This package includes ROS messages and services for dynamixel_workbench packages

dynamixel_interface aims to provide a fast, scalable and easily configurable
    interface for controlling the Robotis 'Dynamixel' brand of servo motors. It
    is implemented in c++ and is based on the official robotis dynamixel_sdk.
    The controller is designed to handle many dynamixels at once and can
    synchronously communicate with dynamixels spread across multiple serial ports.
    The controller can run in either position, velocity or torque control modes
    and is easily configured with an external yaml file.

This package is wrapping version of ROBOTIS Dynamixel SDK for ROS. The ROBOTIS Dynamixel SDK, or SDK, is a software development library that provides Dynamixel control functions for packet communication. The API is designed for Dynamixel actuators and Dynamixel-based platforms.
The DYNAMIXEL SDK ROS example package

Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed. 

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).
     
     They will come as the need arises.

A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to 
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.
Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.
This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.
Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

Any tools relating to mathematical geometry. 
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing 
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these 
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it 
  to be done consistently across platforms.
Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots, 
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include 
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier 
     and are multithread-safe.
Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded 
     programming tools. These are usually different on different 
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support
	
	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.
Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.
Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

Install `/usr/local` (default). ```bash ~/catkin_ws git clone https://github.com/introlab/find-object.git src/find_object_2d catkin_make Run ```bash roscore Launch preferred usb camera driver rosrun uvc_camera uvc_camera_node rosrun find_object_2d find_object_2d image:=image_raw [find_object_2d](http://wiki.ros.org/find_object_2d) information. ROS2 Install Binaries: ```bash Source: ```bash ~/ros2_ws git clone https://github.com/introlab/find-object.git src/find_object_2d colcon build Run ```bash Launch preferred usb camera driver ros2 launch realsense2_camera rs_launch.py Launch find_object_2d node: ros2 launch find_object_2d find_object_2d.launch.py image:=/camera/color/image_raw Draw objects detected image: ros2 run find_object_2d print_objects_detected --ros-args image:=/camera/color/image_raw Pose RGB-D camera required. Realsense camera: ```bash Launch preferred usb camera driver ros2 launch realsense2_camera rs_launch.py align_depth.enable:=true Launch find_object_2d node: ros2 launch find_object_2d find_object_3d.launch.py rgb_topic:=/camera/color/image_raw depth_topic:=/camera/aligned_depth_to_color/image_raw camera_info_topic:=/camera/color/camera_info Show pose camera frame: ros2 run find_object_2d tf_example [find_object_2d](http://wiki.ros.org/find_object_2d) information parameters/topics ROS1 ROS2 versions).

description:
Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by fkie_master_discovery node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network.
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

description: Wraps FMUs for co-simulation
readme: General information repository, including legal information, build instructions issues/limitations, README.md repository root. fmi_adapter package fmi_adapter small ROS package wrapping functional mockup units (FMUs) co-simulation physical models ROS nodes. FMUs defined FMI standard. package supports co-simulation FMUs FMI standard FMUs created variety modeling simulation tools. Examples Dymola, MATLAB/Simulink, OpenModelica, SimulationX, Wolfram System Modeler. Technically, co-simulation FMU zip file suffix .fmu) physical model solver shared library XML file describing inputs, outputs parameters model details solver configuration. addition, zip file source code model solver programming language. fmi_adapter node fmi_adapter ROS node (fmi_adapter_node.cpp), takes FMU creates subscribers publishers input output variables FMU, runs FMU’s solver user-definable update period ROS clock. approach illustrated diagram. fmi_adapter_node searches counterparts FMU parameter variable ROS parameter server initializes FMU correspondingly. purpose, package provide launch file argument fmu_path. Simply call roslaunch fmi_adapter fmi_adapter_node.launch fmu_path:=[PathToTheFMUFile] README.md fmi_adapter_examples package step-by-step description fmi_adapter node damped pendulum model FMU. fmi_adapter library fmi_adapter library convenience functions based common ROS types load FMU runtime, retrieve input, output, parameter names, set timestamped input values, run FMU’s numeric solver, query resulting output. functions class FMIAdapter. Instances class integrated application-specific ROS nodes libraries illustrated architecture diagram. parsing XML description FMU running FMU’s solver, fmi_adapter library FMI Library. library downloaded, compiled linked CMakeLists.txt package cmake’s externalproject_add command. Running FMU ROS node library give code snippets load run FMU file application-specific ROS node library. Step Include FMIAdapter.h fmi_adapter package code. #include "fmi_adapter/FMIAdapter.h" Step Instantiate adapter class path FMU file desired simulation step size. step-size argument omitted, default step size FMU file ros::Duration stepSize(0.001); fmi_adapter::FMIAdapter adapter(fmuPath, stepSize); Step Create subscribers timers set FMU’s input values. ros::Subscriber subscriber nHandle.subscribe<std_msgs::Float64>("angle_x", [&adapter](const std_msgs::Float64::ConstPtr& msg) adapter.setInputValue("angleX", ros::Time::now(), msg->data); angle_x topic angleX input variable FMU. adapter.getInputVariableNames() list input variables. Step Create timer subscriber triggers simulation FMU adapter.doStepsUntil(..). ros::Timer timer nHandle.createTimer(ros::Duration(0.01), [&](const ros::TimerEvent& event) adapter.doStepsUntil(event.current_expected); double adapter.getOutputValue("angleY"); publish topic adapter.getOutputVariableNames() list output variables. Step Set parameters initial values FMU: adapter.setInitialValue("dampingParam", adapter.setInitialValue("angleX", function adapter.initializeFromROSParameters(nodeHandle) initialize parameters ROS parameters. note characters FMU parameter names supported ROS replaced FMIAdapter::rosifyName(name). Step starting spin thread, exit FMU’s initialization mode set ROS time refers FMU’s internal timepoint adapter.exitInitializationMode(ros::Time::now()); ros::spin(); Papers cite repository/package, cite book chapter (PDF Springer Link) File truncated lines full file

description: ROS Foxglove Bridge
readme: foxglove_bridge High performance ROS WebSocket bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, written ROS WebSocket bridge, https://github.com/foxglove/foxglove-sdk. Motivation Live debugging ROS systems traditionally relied running ROS tooling rviz. requires GUI connected peripherals robot, replicating ROS environment network-connected development machine including version ROS, custom message definitions, overcome limitation remote debugging web tooling non-ROS systems, rosbridge developed. rosbridge suffers performance problems high frequency topics and/or large messages, protocol support full visibility ROS systems interacting parameters full graph publishers subscribers. foxglove_bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, similar protocol rosbridge ability support additional schema formats, parameters, graph introspection, non-ROS systems. bridge written designed high performance overhead minimize impact robot stack. Installation Note: binary packages ROS ROS distributions End-of-Life. means binary packages foxglove_bridge released distributions, versions outdated. highly recommended build foxglove_bridge source latest bug fixes. similar reasons, foxglove_bridge package ROS maintenance mode bug fixes applied. Running bridge run bridge node, recommended launch file: ROS roslaunch --screen foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge.launch port:=8765 <launch> Including launch file <include file="$(find foxglove_bridge)/launch/foxglove_bridge.launch"> <arg name="port" arguments </include> </launch> Configuration Parameters configure behavior bridge. parameters set initialization launch file command line, modified runtime. port: TCP port bind WebSocket server valid TCP port number, random port. Defaults address: host address bind WebSocket server Defaults listening interfaces default. Change IPv6) accept connections local machine. tls: true, Transport Layer Security (TLS) encrypted communication. Defaults false. certfile: Path certificate TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults keyfile: Path private key TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted topic names. Defaults service_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted service names. Defaults param_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted parameter names. Defaults client_topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted client-published topic names. Defaults send_buffer_limit: Connection send buffer limit bytes. Messages dropped connection’s send buffer reaches limit avoid queue outdated messages building Defaults use_compression: websocket compression (permessage-deflate). recommended leave turned increases CPU usage per-message compression yields compression ratios robotics data. Defaults false. capabilities: List supported server capabilities. Defaults [clientPublish,parameters,parametersSubscribe,services,connectionGraph,assets]. asset_uri_allowlist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) allowed asset URIs. resource_retriever resolve package://, file:// http(s):// URIs. Note list carefully configured confidential files accidentally exposed websocket connection. extra security measure, URIs consecutive dots disallowed construct URIs retrieval confidential files allowlist configured strict package://<pkg_name>/../../../secret.txt). Defaults ["^package://(?:[-\w%]+/)*[-\w%]+\.(?:dae|fbx|glb|gltf|jpeg|jpg|mtl|obj|png|stl|tif|tiff|urdf|webp|xacro)$"]. max_update_ms: maximum number milliseconds wait polling roscore topics, services, parameters. Defaults service_type_retrieval_timeout_ms: Max number milliseconds retrieving services type information. Defaults Building source Fetch source, install dependencies, build ROS source /opt/ros/noetic/setup.bash mkdir foxglove_bridge_ws/src foxglove_bridge_ws git clone https://github.com/foxglove/ros-foxglove-bridge.git src/ros-foxglove-bridge rosdep update rosdep install --ignore-src --default-yes --from-path src catkin_make install source install/setup.bash Clients Foxglove connects foxglove_bridge live robotics visualization. License foxglove_bridge released MIT license. full terms conditions, LICENSE file.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).
A metapackage to bring in the default packages second generation Transform Library in ros, tf2.
tf2 unit tests

tf2 is the second generation of the transform library, which lets
    the user keep track of multiple coordinate frames over time. tf2
    maintains the relationship between coordinate frames in a tree
    structure buffered in time, and lets the user transform points,
    vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at any desired
    point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description: grep for ROS bag files and live topics: read, filter, export
readme: grepros grep ROS bag files live topics: read, filter, export. Searches ROS messages matches message field regular expression patterns plain text, field type. specific values specific message fields default, matches printed console. Additionally, matches written bagfile HTML/CSV/MCAP/Parquet/Postgres/SQL/SQLite, published live topics. Supports ROS1 ROS2. ROS environment variables set, ROS_VERSION. Supported bag formats: .bag (ROS1), (ROS2), .mcap (ROS1, ROS2). ROS1, messages grepped Python packages message types installed. ROS1 live topics requires ROS master running. ROS2 requires Python packages message types path. Supports loading custom plugins, additional output formats. Usable Python library, LIBRARY.md. Full API documentation https://suurjaak.github.io/grepros. usage Installation Inputs Outputs Matching filtering Plugins Notes ROS1 ROS2 command-line arguments Dependencies Attribution License usage Search text” bags current directory subdirectories: grepros text" Print lines message live ROS topic: grepros --max-per-topic --lines-per-message --live Find message “future” (case-sensitive) my.bag: grepros future --max-count my.bag Find messages, geometry_msgs package, “map” frame, bags current directory, reindexing unindexed bags: grepros frame_id=map --type geometry_msgs/* --max-count --reindex-if-unindexed Pipe diagnostics messages “CPU usage” live ROS topics my.bag: grepros "CPU usage" --type *DiagnosticArray --no-console-output --write my.bag Find messages field “key” topics “diagnostics”, bags “/tmp”: grepros key=0xA002 --topic *diagnostics --path /tmp Find diagnostics_msgs messages bags current directory, “navigation” fields “message”, print header stamp values: grepros --type diagnostic_msgs/* --select-field message --emit-field header.stamp status.values navigation Print message lidar topic ROS1 host highlight: ROS_MASTER_URI=http://1.2.3.4::11311 grepros --live --topic *lidar* --max-per-topic --no-highlight Export bag messages SQLite Postgres, print export progress: grepros my.bag --write my.bag.sqlite --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress grepros my.bag --write postgresql://user@host/dbname --no-console-output --no-verbose --progress Patterns Python regular expression syntax, message matches match. wildcards simple globbing characters, target matches matches. Note expressions quoted avoid shell auto-unescaping auto-expanding linear.x=2.?5 "linear.x=2\.?5". Care unquoted wildcards, auto-expanded shell happen match paths disk. description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
Contains launch files for the hector_geotiff mapper.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.

description:
IRIS package for Localization and Mapping (LaMa).
        This packages includes the sparse-dense library for grid mapping,
        a 2D localization algorithm based on scan matching, an online SLAM solution
        with a dynamic likelihood field and a 2D RB Particle Filter SLAM solution
        with multithreading.
readme: LaMa Localization Mapping library. https://github.com/iris-ua/iris_lama Developed maintained Eurico Pedrosa, University Aveiro Overview LaMa software library robotic localization mapping developed Intelligent Robotics Systems (IRIS) Laboratory University Aveiro Portugal. includes framework volumetric grids mapping), localization algorithm based scan matching SLAM solution Online SLAM Particle Filter SLAM). main feature efficiency. computational effort memory usage minimum viable computer run localization SLAM solutions Raspberry Model Build build LaMa, clone GitHub CMake build. git clone https://github.com/iris-ua/iris_lama iris_lama mkdir build build cmake dependency Eigen3. Note: LaMa provide executable. integration ROS. Integration ROS source code package.xml library external ros packages. provide ROS nodes run localization SLAM solutions. iris_lama_ros information. Sparse-Dense Mapping (SDM) Sparse-Dense Mapping (SDM) framework efficient implementation volumetric grids. divides space small dense patches addressable sparse data-structure. improve memory usage individual patch compressed live operations lossless data compression Zstandard) overhead. replacement OctoMap. grid maps implemented: Distance Map: distance closest occupied cells map. provide DynamicDistanceMap implementation dynamic Euclidean map proposed Lau, Sprunk, Burgard Efficient Grid-Based Spatial Representations Robot Navigation Dynamic Environments Robotics Autonomous Systems, Elsevier Occupancy Map: common representation environment robotics. variants occupancy map SimpleOccupancyMap cell tri-state: free, occupied unknown: ProbabilisticOccupancyMap encodes occupancy probability cell logods; FrequencyOccupancyMap tracks number times beam hits traverses cell calculates hit/miss ratio. information SDM read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Sparse-Dense Approach Efficient Grid Mapping IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC) Localization based Scan Matching provide fast scan matching approach mobile robot localization supported continuous likelihood field. provide accurate localization robots equipped laser good odometry. good odometry recommended. Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Efficient Localization Based Scan Matching Continuous Likelihood Field IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC) Online SLAM environments considerable loops solution accurate efficient. run real time low-spec computer running turtlebot raspberry localization algorithm combined dynamic likelihood field incrementally build occupancy map. information read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Non-Linear Squares Approach SLAM Dynamic Likelihood Field Journal Intelligent Robotic Systems Multi-threaded Particle Filter SLAM Particle Filter SLAM RBPF SLAM GMapping extension Online SLAM solution multiple particles multi-thread support. solution capable parallelizing localization mapping processes. thread-pool manage number working threads. multi-threading, solutions lightweight competitor heavyweight GMapping. information read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Fast Grid SLAM Based Particle Filter Scan Matching Multithreading IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC), Ponta Delgada, Portugal, doi: 10.1109/ICARSC49921.2020.9096191. INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn

Python Package Requirements package supports catkin_virtualenv. pip dependencies written requirements.txt automatically installed virtualenv build. Scripts package list_sesames.py script listing Sesames. Arguments auth_token (required): authorization token. Path file supported. sesame_server.py node controlling Sesame. Parameters ~auth_token (string, required): authorization token. Path file supported. ~device_id (string, optional): UUID Sesame. ~nickname (string, optional): Nickname Sesame. ~device_id ~nickname searching Sesame Sesame list. matches Sesame ~command_timeout (int float, optional): Timeout executing commands. Default [sec]. Advertising services Usage section) ~get_status (sesame_ros/Status) ~lock (sesame_ros/Command) ~unlock (sesame_ros/Command) ~force_sync (sesame_ros/Command) Defined service list sesame_ros/Status string nickname string serial string device_id int32 battery bool locked bool responsive sesame_ros/Command string status bool successful string error Usage list Sesames: run rosrun sesame_ros list_sesames.py YOUR_AUTH_TOKEN controll Sesame: Start service server. description: use switchbot with ros
readme: switchbot_ros package switchbot ROS. System Component JSK members edit original file Setup switchbot Install switchbot app phone(iPhone, Android) create switchbot account. Prepare switchbot devices setup phone. set device enable cloud service. token switchbot App, profile settings, press version times token. JSK members, slide account token details installed switchbots lab. launch switchbot ros Execute roslaunch switchbot_ros switchbot.launch token:=YOUR_TOKEN publish ActionGoal. ROS API switchbot_ros node APIs ~/devices topic Type: switchbot_ros/DeviceArray topic devices list switchbot_ros server. ~/switch action Type: switchbot_ros/SwitchBotcommandAction action provide command interface switchbot devices. command line rostopic pub /switchbot_ros/switch/goal switchbot_ros/SwitchBotCommandActionGoal "header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: goal_id: stamp: secs: nsecs: goal: device_name: '/eng2/7f/73b2/test/button' command: 'press' parameter: command_type: rostopic pub /switchbot_ros/switch/goal switchbot_ros/SwitchBotCommandActionGoal "header: seq: stamp: secs: nsecs: frame_id: goal_id: stamp: secs: nsecs: goal: device_name: '/myroom/light' command: 'turnOn' parameter: command_type: command details. Client libraries rospy/roseus client libraries switchbot_ros server. roseus (setq msg (get-devices)) device list (dolist (device (send msg :devices)) (format type: (send device (send device :type))) print device list (control-device "/eng2/7f/73b2/light/upper/switch" "turnOn") control device (control-device "/eng2/7f/73b2/light/lower/switch" "turnOn") rospy ```python import rospy switchbot_ros.switchbot_ros_client import SwitchBotROSClient rospy.init_node(‘hoge’) client SwitchBotROSClient() devices client.get_devices() print(devices) File truncated lines full file

description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. Subscribing Topics image (sensor_msgs/Image) Input image camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) CameraInfo image. camera info required compute projection matrix drawing markers. image_marker (image_view2/ImageMarker2) Marker topic. image/event (image_view2/MouseEvent) Event interface. image_view2 handle mouse events gui interaction. topic emulate event handling ros topics File truncated lines full file

The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

description: Convert urdf mesh.
readme: URDF Mesh Converter package converts colored output URDF Exporter plugin SolidWorks format readable ROS systems. Specifically, package generates colored .dae models .3dxml files. JSK users refer URDF Exporter documentation guidance. Quick Start building package, source workspace. simply execute command .3dxml URDF file path desired output URDF path. rosrun urdf_mesh_converter convert_urdf_mesh.py <YOUR_URDF_PATH> --output <OUTPUT_URDF_PATH> practical apply package actual URDF running command: rosrun urdf_mesh_converter convert_urdf_mesh.py $(rospack find urdf_mesh_converter)/samples/pitch_yaw_2axis_camera_module.urdf --output $(rospack find urdf_mesh_converter)/samples/pitch_yaw_2axis_camera_module_modified.urdf

Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.
authors: Tully Foote, Radu Bogdan Rusu, William Woodall

description: Library for interfacing to USB devices

The Las Vegas Surface Reconstruction Toolkit is an Open Source toolkit to reconstruct triangle meshes from unordered point clouds. It supports the generation of textured models either using colored point clouds or point clouds plus images and according calibration matrices. The LVR toolkit provides an Open Source C++ API for meshing and texture generation as well as an I/O interface to store the generated meshes in different exchange formats including Stanford PLY, Wavefront OBJ and Collada. In contrast to other meshing software, it focuses on reconstruction of large scale environments on city scale from high resolution point clouds.

readme: MAVLink MAVLink Micro Air Vehicle Message Marshalling Library. MAVLink lightweight, header-only message library communication drones and/or ground control stations. consists primarily message-set specifications systems (“dialects”) defined XML files, Python tools convert source code supported languages. additional Python scripts providing examples utilities working MAVLink data. Tip MAVLink suited applications limited communication bandwidth. reference implementation highly optimized resource-constrained systems limited RAM flash memory. field-proven deployed products serves interoperability interface components manufacturers. Quick start Generate headers install minimal MAVLink environment Ubuntu LTS enter terminal: Dependencies sudo apt install python3-pip Clone mavlink directory choice git clone https://github.com/mavlink/mavlink.git --recursive mavlink python3 pip install pymavlink/requirements.txt build MAVLink2 C-library message_definitions/v1.0/common.xml /mavlink directory shown: python3 pymavlink.tools.mavgen --lang=C --wire-protocol=2.0 --output=generated/include/mavlink/v2.0 message_definitions/v1.0/common.xml cmake include headers cmake, install locally, directory install: cmake -Bbuild -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=install -DMAVLINK_DIALECT=common -DMAVLINK_VERSION=2.0 cmake --build build --target install find_package dependency CMakeLists.txt: find_package(MAVLink REQUIRED) add_executable(my_program my_program.c) target_link_libraries(my_program PRIVATE MAVLink::mavlink) pass local install directory cmake (adapt directory structure): ../my_program cmake -Bbuild -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=../mavlink/install full check examples/c. Note: target_link_libraries cmake, doesn’t “link” MAVLink header-only library. instructions Instructions libraries covered MAVLink Libraries (mavgen). Note: Installing MAVLink Toolchain explains install MAVLink Ubuntu platforms Windows, Generating MAVLink Libraries explains build MAVLink programming languages supported project. sub-topics MAVLink Libraries explain generated libraries. description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl [!NOTE] official ROS EOL, package released Alpine ROS. Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test3.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test3.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting global localization, call /global_localization command. rosservice call /global_localization Demos odometry IMU Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the AbstractPlanner, AbstractController and AbstractRecovery plugin interfaces. This interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make the plugin available for Move Base Flex. The abstract classes provides a meaningful interface enabling the planners, controllers and recovery behaviors to return information, e.g. why something went wrong. Derivided interfaces can, for example, provide methods to initialize the planner, controller or recovery with map representations like costmap_2d, grid_map or other representations.

description: The mbf_abstract_nav package contains the abstract navigation server implementation of Move Base Flex (MBF). The abstract navigation server is not bound to any map representation. It provides the actions for planning, controlling and recovering. MBF loads all defined plugins at the program start. Therefor, it loads all plugins which are defined in the lists *planners*, *controllers* and *recovery_behaviors*. Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the CostmapPlanner, CostmapController and CostmapRecovery interfaces. description: The mbf_utility package

Move Base Flex (MBF) is a backwards-compatible replacement for move_base. MBF can use existing plugins for move_base, and provides an enhanced version of the planner, controller and recovery plugin ROS interfaces. It exposes action servers for planning, controlling and recovering, providing detailed information of the current state and the plugin’s feedback. An external executive logic can use MBF and its actions to perform smart and flexible navigation strategies. Furthermore, MBF enables the use of other map representations, e.g. meshes or grid_map
       
       This package is a meta package and refers to the Move Base Flex stack packages.The abstract core of MBF – without any binding to a map representation – is represented by the

readme: Multiple objects detection, tracking classification LIDAR scans/point-clouds PCL based ROS package Detect/Cluster Track Classify static dynamic objects real-time LIDAR scans implemented Features: tree based point cloud processing object feature detection point clouds Unsupervised euclidean cluster extraction k-means clustering based detected features refinement RANSAC Stable tracking (object data association) ensemble Kalman Filters Robust compared k-means clustering mean-flow tracking Usage: Follow steps (multi_object_tracking_lidar) package: Create catkin workspace setup Navigate src folder catkin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src Clone repository: git clone https://github.com/praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar.git Compile build package: ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Add catkin workspace ROS environment: source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Run kf_tracker ROS node package: rosrun multi_object_tracking_lidar kf_tracker ROS node running! long point clouds published filtered_cloud rostopic, outputs node published obj_id, cluster_0, cluster_1, cluster_5 topics markers topic visualize RViz. Supported point-cloud streams/sources: input point-clouds real LiDAR simulated LiDAR point cloud dataset data source produces point clouds Note: package expects valid point cloud data input. point clouds publish “filtered_cloud” expected NaNs. point cloud filtering task application dependent module. description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

        a convex polygon or cicrle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters
        that can be set in a launch file. The parameters for this planner are also
        dynamically reconfigurable. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the
        BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in

description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.
Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.
