description:
Integration of the Arduino Nicla Vision board in the ROS world. 
    Sensors data from the Nicla Vision is collected and trasmited to the relevant ROS topics
authors: Davide Torielli, Damiano Gasperini, Edoardo Del Bianco, Federico Rollo
readme: Nicla Vision ROS package :rocket: Check ROS2 version: Nicla Vision ROS2 repository :rocket: Description ROS package enables Arduino Nicla Vision board ready-to-use ROS world! :boom: implemented architecture image: Arduino Nicla Vision board streams sensors data ROS-running machine TCP/UDP socket Serial, UART USB cable. package running ROS-running machine, allowing deserialize received info, stream ROS topics list sensors respective ROS topics: color camera streams /nicla/camera/camera_info /nicla/camera/image_raw /nicla/camera/image_raw/compressed Time-of-Flight (distance) sensor streams /nicla/tof Microphone streams /nicla/audio /nicla/audio_info /nicla/audio_stamped /nicla/audio_recognized Imu streams /nicla/imu user easily configure package, launch parameters, receive sensors data UDP TCP socket connections, socket address. user decide sensor streamed ROS environment. repository find Python code optimised receiving data board, subsequently publishing ROS topics. Table Contents Installation Usage Package List Video License Cite Installation Step-by-step instructions ROS package running Binaries noetic: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-nicla-vision-ros ROS2, check https://github.com/ADVRHumanoids/nicla_vision_ros2.git Source installation Usual catkin build <your_workpace>/src git clone https://github.com/ADVRHumanoids/nicla_vision_ros.git <your_workpace> catkin build source <your_workpace>/devel/setup.bash Arduino Nicla Vision setup completed setup steps Nicla Vision Drivers repository, turn Arduino Nicla Vision. power Arduino Nicla Vision, automatically connect network start streaming ROS-running machine. Optional Audio Recognition VOSK run speech recognition feature module, publish recognized words /nicla/audio_recognized topic. moment, VOSK utilized. Arduino version supported. VOSK setup pip install vosk Download VOSK model https://alphacephei.com/vosk/models Check recognition arguments nicla_receiver.launch file Usage Follow steps enjoying Arduino Nicla Vision board ROS! Run ROS package Launch package: roslaunch nicla_vision_ros nicla_receiver.launch receiver_ip:="x.x.x.x" connection_type:="tcp/udp/serial" <optional arguments> Set socket type TCP, UDP Serial (`connection_type:=tcp`, `udp`, `serial`). Set `receiver_ip` address ROS-running machine. address executing command: ifconfig "inet" address "enp" voice. *Note* argument `connection_type:="tcp/udp/serial` `<optional arguments>`, decide: sensor streamed ROS `enable_imu:=true enable_range:=true enable_audio:=true enable_audio_stamped:=false enable_camera_compressed:=true enable_camera_raw:=true` Serial connection, set `camera_receive_compressed` nicla sending images (COMPRESS_IMAGE) socket port (default `receiver_port:=8002`). Serial connection, `receiver_port` argument port board `receiver_port:=/dev/ttyACM0` run ready receiving sensors data File truncated lines full file

description:
The nodelet package is designed to provide a way to run multiple
    algorithms in the same process with zero copy transport between
    algorithms.

    This package provides both the nodelet base class needed for
    implementing a nodelet, as well as the NodeletLoader class used
    for instantiating nodelets.

description: NTRIP client that will publish RTCM corrections to a ROS topic, and optionally subscribe to NMEA messages to send to an NTRIP server

Build Instructions Building source Install ROS create workspace: Installing Configuring ROS Environment Move entire ntrip_client folder your_workspace/src directory. Install rosdeps package: rosdep install --from-paths ~/your_workspace/src --ignore-src Build workspace: ~/your_workspace catkin_make source ~/your_workspace/devel/setup.bash source command run terminal prior launching ROS node. Launch node publish data command launch node. mind instance run separate terminal. roslaunch ntrip_client ntrip_client.launch Optional launch parameters: host: Hostname address NTRIP server connect port: Port connect server. Default: mountpoint: Mountpoint connect NTRIP server. ntrip_version: Ntrip-Version header initial HTTP request caster. authenticate: authenticate server, send unauthenticated request. set true, username, password supplied. username: Username authenticating NTRIP server. authenticate true password: Password authenticating NTRIP server. authenticate true ssl: connect SSL. cert, key, ca_cert options effect true cert: NTRIP caster configured cert based authentication, option client certificate key: NTRIP caster configured cert based authentication, option private key ca_cert: NTRIP caster signed certs, chain, option file rtcm_message_packege: type ROS RTCM message published node. description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.

description: Mapping tools to be used with the

octomap_server loads a 3D map (as Octree-based OctoMap) and distributes it to other nodes in a compact binary format. It also allows to incrementally build 3D OctoMaps, and provides map saving in the node octomap_saver.

description: Ethernet interface to OxTS GPS receivers (NCOM packet structure)
readme: Ethernet interface OxTS GPS receivers NCOM packet structure Tested RT3000v2 receiver. Published Topics gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) GPS position geodetic coordinates (latitude longitude) gps/vel (geometry_msgs/TwistWithCovarianceStamped) GPS velocity ENU frame linear.x Velocity due East linear.y Velocity due North linear.z Velocity gps/odom (nav_msgs/Odometry) GPS position heading UTM frame, velocity vehicle body frame pose.position.x UTM Easting meters pose.position.y UTM Northing meters pose.orientation Yaw quaternion representing heading UTM. mind UTM heading relative UTM grid, necessarily aligned due East. phenomenon “grid convergence”. Wikipedia details. twist.linear.x Local frame velocity vehicle twist.linear.y Local frame lateral velocity vehicle twist.angular.z Yaw rate vehicle rad/s imu/data (sensor_msgs/Imu) Orientation, angular rates, linear accelerations reported OxTS unit gps/pos_type (std_msgs/String) Position solution type reported OxTS unit (point position, differential pseudorange, RTK float, RTK integer, gps/nav_status (std_msgs/String) Navigation status reported OxTS unit. fix, vel, odom, imu/data topics published topic string “READY”. gps/time_ref (sensor_msgs/TimeReference) UTC equivalent GPS timestamp reported OxTS unit. Parameters interface Restrict single network interface, eth0. Default <empty> ip_address Local listen address. Default <empty> port Local UDP listen port. Default frame_id_gps frame-ID geodetic GPS position IMU. Default gps frame_id_vel frame-ID ENU GPS velocity. Default enu frame_id_odom frame-ID UTM odometry. Default utm usage rosrun oxford_gps_eth gps_node roslaunch oxford_gps_eth gps.launch FAQ Connected Oxford GPS <ip_address>:3000 GPS position velocity messages published. Data published OxTS unit reports completely initialized. Drive faster short period time powering OxTS unit initialize sensor. gps/nav_status string topic status initialization process. string shows “READY”, GPS position velocity messages start publishing.

readme: Pinocchio instantiates state-of-the-art Rigid Body Algorithms poly-articulated systems based revisited Roy Featherstone’s algorithms. Pinocchio analytical derivatives main Rigid-Body Algorithms, Recursive Newton-Euler Algorithm Articulated-Body Algorithm. Pinocchio originally designed robotics applications, contexts (biomechanics, computer graphics, vision, built Eigen linear algebra FCL collision detection. Pinocchio includes Python interface fast code prototyping, accessible Conda. Pinocchio heart robotics software Crocoddyl, open-source efficient Differential Dynamic Programming solver robotics, Stack-of-Tasks, open-source versatile hierarchical controller framework, Humanoid Path Planner, open-source software Motion Manipulation Planning. learn Pinocchio internal behaviors main features, invite read related paper online documentation DeepWiki. dive Pinocchio single line sufficient (assuming Conda): conda install pinocchio conda-forge pip Linux): pip install pin Table contents Table contents Pinocchio main features Documentation Examples Tutorials Pinocchio continuous integrations Performances Ongoing developments Installation ROS Visualization Citing Pinocchio Questions Issues Core-dev team Credits Open-source projects relying Pinocchio Acknowledgments Pinocchio main features Pinocchio fast: template library, cache friendly, support custom scalar type. Pinocchio versatile, implementing basic advanced rigid body dynamics algorithms: kinematics analytical derivatives, forward/inverse dynamics analytical derivatives, centroidal dynamics analytical derivatives, computations kinematic dynamic regressors system identification full support closed-loop mechanisms, state-of-the-art frictional contact solvers, low-complexity constrained articulated body algorithms, sparse constrained dynamics analytical derivatives, full support multiple-precision floating-point (MPFR) Python support modern open-source Automatic Differentiation frameworks CppAD CasADi, automatic code generation support CppADCodeGen. Pinocchio support description formats: URDF format, SDF format, MJCF format, SRDF format, programmatically. Pinocchio flexible: header template instantiation, compliant. Communicating PointCloud2 messages point_cloud_transport: #include <ros/ros.h> #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.h> void Callback(const sensor_msgs::PointCloud2ConstPtr& msg) process message ros::NodeHandle point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(nh); point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct.subscribe("in_point_cloud_base_topic", Callback); point_cloud_transport::Publisher pub pct.advertise("out_point_cloud_base_topic", Republish node(let) Similar image_transport, package node(let) called republish convert transports. launch file <node name="republish" pkg="point_cloud_transport" type="republish" args="draco raw in:=input_topic out:=output_topic" transports draco_point_cloud_transport: Lossy compression Google Draco library. write Don’t hesitate send pull request adding list! description:
draco_point_cloud_transport provides a plugin to point_cloud_transport for sending point clouds
    encoded with KD tree compression.
readme: DRACO POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT Plugin ROS package point_cloud_transport, Google Draco compression library low-bandwidth lossy transportation PointCloud2 messages. Publisher plugin dynamic reconfiguration parameters, change compression runtime. Draco encode invalid values, pointclouds invalid values turned unstructured dense clouds NaNs, height Encode Decode Speed adjusting encode_speed dedode_speed parameters, adjust speed plugin encodes point cloud. higher number, higher speed encoding/decoding, worse compression. detailed information, documentation Draco. Encode Method Auto method decides encoding method based parameter default method. KD-tree method forces encoder KD-tree encoding. attribute point cloud type float32, kd-tree encoding requires attribute quantized. Sequential method forces encoder sequential encoding. Quantization sequential encoding. Sequential encoding worse compression KD-tree, faster arrangement points point cloud intact. sequential encoding encode point clouds Kinect. Deduplicate Deduplicate option tells encoder delete duplicate points point cloud, allowing transport smaller point clouds. Force Quantization Force_quantization option forces quantization KD-tree encoding method. Quantization Attribute Types Quantization_POSITION, Quantization_NORMAL, Quantization_COLOR tells encoder bits quantization attribute type. Attribute type point cloud attribute recognized based list names: POSITION POSITION POSITION “pos” POSITION “position” POSITION “rgb” COLOR “rgba” COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR NORMAL NORMAL NORMAL encoded GENERIC custom quantization, edit list recognized names expert_quantization expert_attribute_type options. Expert Quantization Expert_quantization option encoder custom quantization values point cloud attributes. Multiple POSITION attribute encoded varying quantization levels. set quantization PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x expert_quantization, user quantization bits PointField entries point cloud. Expert Attribute Types Expert_attribute_types option encoder custom attribute types encoding point cloud attributes. set type PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/attribute_type/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/attribute_type/x "'POSITION'" expert_attribute_types, user type PointField entries point cloud. Accepted types POSITION NORMAL COLOR TEX_COORD GENERIC encoding rgb/rgba COLOR, user common rgba tweak ROS (encoding rgba instances Byte instance float32). inform encoder, PointField entry “rgb” handled tweak, set parameter: rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/rgba_tweak/rgb true Subscriber Set Skip Dequantization Attribute Types SkipDequantizationPOSITION, SkipDequantizationNORMAL, SkipDequantizationCOLOR options decoder skip dequantization attribute types.

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

description: Python wrapper for Mobile Robot Programming Toolkit (MRPT) libraries

This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Dorian Scholz, Dirk Thomas

qt_gui provides the infrastructure for an integrated graphical user interface based on Qt.

description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

Launch files to open an RGBD device and load all nodelets to 
     convert raw depth/RGB/IR streams to depth images, disparity images, 
     and (registered) point clouds.

description: The robot_navigation package

The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.

description:
Tools for directing, throttling, selecting, and otherwise messing with
    ROS topics at a meta level. None of the programs in this package actually
    know about the topics whose streams they are altering; instead, these
    tools deal with messages as generic binary blobs. This means they can be
    applied to any ROS topic.

description: Metapackage for Cartesian ROS controllers

A ros_control controller accepting Cartesian twist messages in order to move a robot manipulator.
    It uses a Cartesian interface to the robot, so that the robot hardware takes care about
    doing the inverse kinematics. This could be used e.g. for visual servoing applications.
readme: Twist Controller Cartesian ROS-controller commanding target twists robot. controller TwistCommandInterface set user twist message reference robot control. Controller .yaml configuration twist_controller: type: ros_controllers_cartesianlers/TwistController controller identifier command handle hardware interface. frame_id: "endpoint_to_control" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 Acknowledgement Developed collaboration Supported ROSIN ROS-Industrial Quality-Assured Robot Software Components. information: rosin-project.eu project received funding European Union’s Horizon research innovation programme grant agreement

Usage Create YAML config file mappings format: republishers: topic: "/fruits_per_cubic_m" msg_type: "fruit_msgs/Citrus" mappings: field: "num_oranges" mapping_type: "single_field" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "oranges" topic: "/hardware/status" msg_type: "hw_msgs/HardwareStatus" mappings: field: "status" mapping_options: fields: ["lidar", "motor", "battery"] filter: 'lambda (x.status mapping_type: "array_of_fields" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "hardware_error" topic: "/cmd_vel" msg_type: "geometry_msgs/Twist" mappings: field: "linear" mapping_type: "json_of_fields" mapping_options: fields: topic: "/inorbit/linear_vel_test" key: "linear_vel" topic: "/map_metadata" latched: true msg_type: "nav_msgs/MapMetaData" mappings: field: "resolution" mapping_type: "single_field" topic: "/inorbit/map_res_test" key: "map_resolution" topic: "/navsat" msg_type: "sensor_msgs/NavSatFix" mappings: mapping_type: "serialize" mapping_options: fields: ["status", "latitude", "longitude", "position_covariance_type"] topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "navsat" static_publishers: fixed string" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "greeting" value_from: environment_variable: "PATH" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "env_path" launch republisher.py script passing config file config param. suggested organize creating config file launch file package, launch file <launch> <node name="inorbit_republisher" pkg="inorbit_republisher" type="republisher.py"> <param name="config" textfile="$(find inorbit_republisher)/config/example.yaml" </node> </launch> Mapping ROS topics republisher map ROS values single field 'fruit=apple') array fields 'fruits=[{fruit1: apple, fruit2: orange}, {fruit1: melon, fruit2: apple}]'). capture simple fields data array values. Single field: mapping options republishing single field, include set mapping_options mapping. include: mapping_type: mapping option set single_field. filter: lambda expression control published based condition. you’d republish String values SPAMMY STRING, mapping_options: filter: 'lambda "SPAMMY STRING")' Array fields: mapping options File truncated lines full file

description: Contains aligned allocation functions, as well as an STL-compatible AlignedAllocator class.

The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.
The ros_realtime package

rosatomic provides the C++11-style atomic operations by pulling symbols from the proposed Boost.Atomic
     package into the ros namespace.  Once C++11-style atomics (std::atomic) are available from compilers, rosatomic will
     conditionally use those instead.
rosrt provides classes for interfacing with ROS from within realtime systems, such as realtime-safe Publisher and Subscriber classes.

Dependencies installation Build essentials cmake sudo apt-get install build-essential cmake sudo apt-get install qt5-default ROS (Robot Operative System) Follow ROS distro installation guide: Melodic Noetic (Optional) Clang-Format sudo add-apt-repository 'deb http://apt.llvm.org/focal/ llvm-toolchain-focal main' sudo apt update sudo apt install clang-format-16 Usage Clone repository ROS workspace compile cmake: mkdir your_ros_ws/src your_ros_ws/src git clone https://github.com/fada-catec/rosbag_rviz_panel catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Source workspace launch RViz: source devel/setup.zsh rviz Add custom panel RViz layout: Click “Panels” tab RViz. description: Converts between Python dictionaries and JSON to rospy messages.
readme: rospy_message_converter Rospy_message_converter lightweight ROS package Python library convert Python dictionaries JSON messages rospy messages, vice versa. ROS ROS branches ROS users master branch. ROS users branch distro (foxy/galactic/humble/rolling/…). Usage Convert dictionary ROS message rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String dictionary 'data': 'Howdy' message message_converter.convert_dictionary_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', dictionary) Convert ROS message dictionary rospy_message_converter import message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Howdy') dictionary message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_dictionary(message) Convert JSON ROS message rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String json_str '{"data": "Hello"}' message json_message_converter.convert_json_to_ros_message('std_msgs/String', json_str) Convert ROS message JSON rospy_message_converter import json_message_converter std_msgs.msg import String message String(data 'Hello') json_str json_message_converter.convert_ros_message_to_json(message) Test run tests: catkin_make test pre-commit Formatting Checks repo pre-commit check runs locally set run automatically commit install, pip: pip3 install --user pre-commit run files repo manually: pre-commit run run pre-commit automatically committing local repo, install git hooks: pre-commit install License Project released BSD license. readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. File truncated lines full file

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

description: serl_franka_controllers provides a compliant yet accurate Cartesian impedance controller for controlling Franka Emika research robots that can be used in online reinforcement learning applications
readme: SERL Franka Controllers Robot controller SERL Software Suite Sample-Efficient Robotic Reinforcement Learning) Serl Website Paper: https://serl-robot.github.io/ serl_franka_controllers ROS package designed control Franka Emika Robot libfranka franka_ros. package compliant accurate Cartesian Impedance Controller safe online reinforcement learning algorithms, Joint Position Controller resetting arm. Compliance accuracy achieved time limiting reference point Impedance controller distance current pose realtime loop. high gain accuracy excess force contact. Installation Prerequisites ROS Noetic Installation libfranka>=0.8.0 franka_ros>=0.8.0 Franka FCI Documentation sudo apt install ros-noetic-libfranka ros-noetic-franka-ros Installing apt-get sudo apt-get install ros-serl_franka_controllers Installing Source ~/catkin_ws/src git clone git@github.com:rail-berkeley/serl_franka_controllers.git ~/catkin_ws catkin_make --pkg serl_franka_controllers source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Realtime Constraint franka_ros requires realtime kernel default. install CUDA machine. workaround ignore realtime constraint catkin_ws/src/franka_ros/franka_control/config/franka_control_node.yaml realtime_config: ignore Usage Cartesian Impedance Controller launch Cartesian Impedance Controller, roslaunch serl_franka_controllers impedance.launch robot_ip:=<RobotIP> load_gripper:=<true/false> Replace address Franka robot. load_gripper argument boolean (true false) depending gripper attached. Compliance parameters controller adjusted interactive GUI running rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure. achieved Python code demonstrated section. Joint Position Controller resetting moving robot specific joint position, launch joint position controller: rosparam set /target_joint_positions roslaunch serl_franka_controllers joint.launch robot_ip:=<RobotIP> load_gripper:=<true/false> replace actual address load_gripper option. replace desired joint positions. rospy include requirements.txt python script show interacting controller. script shows adjust reference limiting values send robot commands ROS Topics dynamic_reconfigure. run conda create serl_controller python=3.8 conda activate serl_controller pip install requirements.txt python test/test.py --robot_ip=ROBOT_IP

description: Package for using SkyWay from ROS
readme: SkyWay ROS ROSからSkyWayを利用するためのパッケージです。 端末間でP2P接続を行うための規格としてWebRTCが標準化されています。 SkyWayはWebRTCプラットフォームであり、SkyWayを利用することで、ブラウザ・iOS・Androidなどの環境で相互にWebRTC接続を簡単に行うことができます。 WebRTCは映像と音声を双方向通信するためのMediaStreamと、データを転送するためのDataChannelが定義されています。 Skyway ROSを利用することで、MediaStreamとDataChannelをROSパッケージから利用することができます。 SkyWay ROSでは2つのサービスが提供されており、これらを利用し操作を行うことで、外部プログラム(Peer)に対してMediaStreamとDataConnection(over DataChannel)の確立を行います。 SkyWayControl SkyWayEvents SkyWay ROS内のMediaSocketに対してRTP/RTCPを送信することで、確立されたMedia Connectionを経由して、Peerに対して映像と音声を送ることができます。 また、データ転送用のROS Pluginをユーザ側で定義することができ、そのPluginに対してROS Topic, Service, Actionなどを用いてデータを送受信することで、 Peerとデータのやりとりを行うことができます。 操作方法とAPI仕様 まずSkyWay ROS内に、SkyWayサーバとやり取りするために　Peer Objectを生成します。以下のドキュメントを参照してください。 PeerObjectの生成 PeerObjectの状態確認 Peer Objectが生成できたら、MediaConnection, DataConnectionの接続処理及び待ち受けが行えるようになります。以下のページを参照してください。 MediaConnectionの確立 MediaConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionの確立 DataConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionが確立できたら、Pluginを介して外部ROS Moduleとデータのやり取りを行えます。 Pluginの仕様についてはこちらのドキュメントを参照してください。 tipsも参照して下さい。 examplesディレクトリ及びskyway_for_ros_examplesリポジトリを参照して下さい。

description:
This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

This package pulls in the Karto mapping library, and provides a ROS
     wrapper for using it.

Parameters ~agent_address (str, default Address SNMP agent. ~agent_port (int, default UDP port SNMP agent. ~community (str, default ‘public’): SNMP community. ~snmp_v2 (bool, default True): SNMPv2c SNMPv1. ~udpv6 (bool, default False): UDPv6 transport UDPv4. ~rate (float, default rate SNMP agent polled. ~plugins (dict): Plugin-specific configuration. Keys config_key attributes plugins if_mib). Values dictionaries plugin. MIB Processing Plugins single plugin, planned added host resources). if_mib plugin reads IF-MIB extracts information network ports status. Parameters num_ports (int, optional): diagnostics check number ports reported. ports (dict port_name port_params): Specification expected ports. port_name string identifying port eth0), mangled ifAlias, ifName ifDescr port_params dict keys: connected (bool, optional): Check connection state port speed (int tuple (int, int), optional): expected speed port Mbps. tuple denotes (min, max) range. mtu (int tuple (int, int), optional): expected MTU port tuple denotes (min, max) range. dummy port present, require properties state, dummy parameter port’s key present ports dictionary. port_name mangled valid ROS graph resource matching regex ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$. perform mangling, iconv //TRANSLIT feature find “closest” ASCII character non-ASCII non-alphanumeric characters replaced underscores spaces), multiple underscores coalesced single resulting valid graph resource starts number), “source” order ifAlias, ifName, ifDescr. Writing plugin create plugin processing MIBs, write normal Python ROS package exports Python package plugin. plugin inherit class snmp_diagnostics.SnmpDiagPlugin implement abstract methods. Add lines package’s package.xml: <exec_depend>snmp_diagnostics</exec_depend> <export> <snmp_diagnostics class="my_package.module.Class" config_key="my_plugin" </export> my_package.module.Class fully qualified plugin class. config_key configure plugin ROS parameter server, configuration ~/plugins/<config_key>. description: Plugin-replacement for the default costmap_2d::InflationLayer.
readme: Small-Objects-Big (Sob) Layer SobLayer offers faster alternative commonly costmap_2d::InflationLayer. implements distance transform algorithm Felzenszwalb Huttenlocher Install/Build you’re ros-noetic, install package apt: sudo apt install ros-noetic-sob-layer you’re older ros-version build package sources, follow “normal” build process catkin-based packages: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/dorezyuk/sob_layer.git catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release additional performance boost compiling optimization, enables auto-vectorization: catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="-O3 -march=native" library offers benchmark-target. benchmarking figure SobLayer offers reasonable improvement costmap_2d::InflationLayer specific usecase/platform. benchmark-target requires benchmark library. build add -Dsob_layer_BENCHMARK=ON build command: catkin build sob_layer --no-deps --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -Dsob_layer_BENCHMARK=ON Requirements library requires tested Ubuntu Ubuntu running ros-melodic ros-noetic, compilers work GCC Clang Benchmarks library benchmarked costmap_2d::InflationLayer. benchmarks focus extreme scenarios. scenario measure performance large, connected obstacles. place filled square middle map. square set size NxN. ranges times map’s edge (assuming map square size ExE). increasing increases “load-factor” (number occupied cells). scenario measure performance unconnected obstacles. flattened map, mark N-th cell occupied. decreasing values cells marked occupied “load-factor” increases. N-values image shows results. results obtained AMD Ryzen PRO CPU. library compiled GCC -march=native” flags. y-axis cpu-time. x-axis shows “load-factor” (increasing left scenarios run map-sizes: upper row shows results smaller size, larger. Config SobLayer configuration costmap_2d::InflationLayer, easing change users. <common|local|global>_costmap.yaml plugins: inflation_layer, type: "sob_layer::SobLayer"} inflation_layer: meters. positive suppress automatic calculation footprint inflation_radius: meters, costmap_2d::InflationLayer inscribed_radius: decay, costmap_2d::InflationLayer cost_scaling_factor: behavior costmap_2d::Layer: calls updateCosts updateBounds skipped, false enabled: true set true, costs costmap_2d::NO_INFORMATION overwritten cost larger costmap_2d::FREE_SPACE. inflate_unknown: true File truncated lines full file

description:
The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional argument <arg name="output" value="screen" optional argument </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

description: Efficient Task Space Inverse Dynamics (TSID) based on Pinocchio
readme: TSID Task Space Inverse Dynamics TSID library optimization-based inverse-dynamics control based rigid multi-body dynamics library Pinocchio. Documentation project wiki overview design library. exercises folder find examples TSID Python robot manipulators, humanoids, quadrupeds. website Andrea Del Prete find slides video lessons TSID. Memmo summer school Installation Conda dive TSID Python, single line sufficient (assuming Conda installed): conda install tsid conda-forge Installation Debian/Ubuntu packages, robotpkg added robotpkg’s software repository commands: sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/robotpkg.list <<EOF deb [arch=amd64] http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/pub $(lsb_release robotpkg EOF curl http://robotpkg.openrobots.org/packages/debian/robotpkg.key sudo apt-key add sudo apt update install TSID python bindings (replace Python version) sudo apt install robotpkg-py3*-tsid Installation sources install dependencies: boost (unit_test_framework) eigen3 pinocchio eiquadprog example-robot-data running examples) install eigen3 Ubuntu apt-get: sudo apt-get install libeigen3-dev install pinocchio follow instruction website. compile TSID: $DEVEL/openrobots/src/ git clone --recursive git@github.com:stack-of-tasks/tsid.git tsid mkdir _build-RELEASE _build-RELEASE cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RELEASE -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=$DEVEL/openrobots install Python Bindings library python, offer python bindings based Boost.Python EigenPy. install EigenPy compile source code: git clone https://github.com/stack-of-tasks/eigenpy Ubuntu, apt-get: sudo apt-get install robotpkg-py3*-eigenpy testing python bindings, run unit test scripts script folder, instance: ipython script/test_formulation.py run demo gepetto-viewer: ipython demo/demo_romeo.py Credits package authored Andrea Del Prete (University Trento) Justin Carpentier (INRIA) includes key contributions Julian Viereck (Max Planck Institute, York University) Sanghyun Kim (Seoul National University) Eloise Dalin (LORIA, INRIA Lorraine) Noelie Ramuzat (LAAS, CNRS) Pierre Fernbach (LAAS, CNRS) Aurelie Bonnefoy (LAAS, CNRS) File truncated lines full file

This repository includes ros packages to plan routes for multiple robots on a search graph. It creates a search graph out of a pixel map and tries to find a path for multiple robots using an extended approach for prioritized planning. The inputs are the tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RobotInfo messages which include the robots pose, the map and the desired goal poses. The output are multiple synchronized routes given to the individual robots.
maintainers: Benjamin Binder, Markus Bader, Florian Beck

description:
The tuw_multi_robot_goal_generator package was designed to generate, store, and read goal lists for the tuw_multi_robot_router.
    The goal_saver is able to store publish goal msgs of type tuw_multi_robot_msgs::RobotGoalsArray in a human readable form. 
    The goal_server is able to read and publish this msgs and to read patches of saved goals for testing.
    The goals_random node subscribes to the occupancy grid and is able to auto generate valid goals for testing.

description:
This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS
    Image messages and OpenCV images.

`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.
Packages for interfacing ROS with OpenCV, a library of programming functions for real time computer vision.
Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode, Flash code, or April tag pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode, Flash code April tag pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode, Flash April tag code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. description: Implementation of warehouse_ros for sqlite
readme: SQLite backend warehouse_ros storage backend warehouse_ros SQLite. sqlite file ROS parameter warehouse_host. warehouse_port Installation Create folder catkin workspace. Create file named warehouse.rosinstall content: git: local-name: warehouse_ros_sqlite uri: https://github.com/ros-planning/warehouse_ros_sqlite.git version: master open terminal navigate folder. wstool catkin_tools installed. Initialize workspace fetch source files wstool: rosdep update wstool init src wstool merge src warehouse.rosinstall wstool update src Install missing dependencies: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build workspace catkin: catkin config --install --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release catkin build activate workspace: source install/setup.bash plugin installed, refer warehouse_ros documentation usage interface test files test/. Adapt .launch files plugin MoveIt stack, adapt .launch files. plugin loaded warehouse_ros_mongo. Remember set db:=True roslaunch command. you’re RViz, you’ll enter path database file Host field click connect. warehouse.launch Run server <node name="$(anon mongo_wrapper_ros)" cwd="ROS_HOME" type="mongo_wrapper_ros.py" pkg="warehouse_ros_mongo"> <param name="overwrite" value="false"/> <param name="database_path" value="$(arg moveit_warehouse_database_path)" </node> warehouse_settings.launch.xml default host moveit <arg name="moveit_warehouse_host" default="localhost" <arg name="moveit_warehouse_host" default="/path/to/your/file.sqlite" Set parameters warehouse <param name="warehouse_port" value="$(arg moveit_warehouse_port)"/> <param name="warehouse_host" value="$(arg moveit_warehouse_host)"/> <param name="warehouse_plugin" value="warehouse_ros_mongo::MongoDatabaseConnection" <param name="warehouse_plugin" value="warehouse_ros_sqlite::DatabaseConnection"
